creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to wen from comment #29)

I understand Wen, but we can't leave known vulnerable versions not documented as such. For whoever takes care of ports updates, the responsibility is with them to either take care of the required changes, or obtain help/support from others to do so. Otherwise too many things end up falling through the cracks.

Also, for future updates, could make sure we tag PR: numbers on commits *and* merges so these changes are tagged on their relevent issues? It's really, really hard to figure out what's happened so far, and whats left to do if anything, without them.

@Ports-Secteam Can you help with vuxml entries?

^Triage: 

- py-numpy merged to 2022Q2 in ports 03a6fffb8ef8 (and followup checksum fix in ports c7535f6bc2ae)
